{{Quote|I know that Salek ''[sic]'' is still alive somewhere. If anyone has any information, or any of my fans are willing to donate credits to the search, please contact the Salek-Come-Home ''[sic]'' HoloNet registry.|Jimke Weet|<ref name="hnn" />}}
'''Jimke Weet''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] renowned [[Kloo horn/Legends|kloo horn]] soloist and the [[Parent/Legends|father]] of [[Salak Weet]]. When Salak went missing<ref name="hnn" /> in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]]<ref name="date">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|regional/13328_2.html|Missing Starship Search Abandoned}} is dated to [[Great ReSynchronization|13:3:28]], which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]]. Since [[Salak Weet]]'s voyage occurred four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, it must have occurred in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]].</ref> during the first voyage of him and his friend, [[Noa Briqualon]], Jimke funded a massive four-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-long rescue search for the pair, in which they spent nearly every [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] they owned, in an attempt to find them. The last known responder signal from Salak and Noa's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] had been near the [[Monsua Nebula/Legends|Monsua Nebula]], an area of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] known for [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] activity. The search was eventually called off<ref name="hnn" /> in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="date" /> however, following Jimke's filing for [[Bank/Legends|bankruptcy]]. Following the end of the search, Jimke expressed hope of Salak's survival in a quote from the [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|CoCo District Edition]] of the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], published on the date [[Great ReSynchronization|13:3:28]].<ref name="hnn" /> At some point, Weet made an album of anti-war [[Music/Legends|music]] with the [[Musician/Legends|musician]] [[Orin Mersai]] and the duo [[Kang and Lubrichs]].<ref name="slugthrowers">{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-an-overview-of-popular-music-and-musicians-in-a-galaxy-far-far-away-part-1|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Jimke Weet was created for [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 49|''HoloNet News Vol. 531 #49'']], an issue of the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] website published online<ref name="hnn" /> on [[March 28]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN49 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6217551|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=Dev Sibwarra|date=2002-03-28|time=4:24 PM|archivedate=20200305125429}}</ref> It was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]].<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref>
